result.markdown: --- execute: # Default settings for code blocks warning: false # Do not print warnings message: false # Do not print messages size: "scriptsize" # Set code size to script size format: pdf: # Specify .pdf formatting options documentclass: article # Document Class number-sections: true # Number Sections reference-section-title: "References" # Title for reference section code-line-numbers: true # Line numbers printed in code cap-location: top # Captions at the top of tables geometry: - top=0.75in - left=0.75in - bottom=0.75in - right=0.75in format-links: [pdf] --- \normalsize ::: {.cell} ::: \normalsize # Ingredients \normalsize - 1.5 cups flour - 0.75 cups sugar - 2 tsp baking powder - 1/4 tsp salt - 1/3 cup avocado oil - 1 large egg - 1/3 cup milk - 2 teaspoons vanilla extract - 1 cup blueberries (any berry can work) - 2 green onions - 1 large poblano - 2 roma tomatoes - 1 can of black beans - 1 tablespoon southwest spice - 6-8 flour tortillas - 1 jar of salsa verde - 4 oz monterey jack cheese - 2 tablespoons sour cream - 1 yellow onion - 3 celery stalks - 1 lb gold potatoes - 4 oz mascarpone cheese - 2 teaspoons white miso (can use tamari or soy sauce instead) - 16 oz salmon - 1/4 oz dill - 1 large Vidalia onion - 6 garlic cloves - 12 oz bag of peas - 1 pound regular or mezzi rigatoni - 1/2 cup grated parmesan cheese - Italian seasoning and chili flakes \normalsize \newpage # Recipe One \normalsize 1. Dice tomato and poblano. Thinly slice green onions, separating greens and whites. Drain (save liquid) and rinse black beans. 1. Cook poblano in oil over medium heat until softened (5-7 minutes). 1. Add almost all of the southwest seasoning (except a half tsp), tomato, green onion whites, half the black beans, and 1/8 cup black bean liquid. Cook another 5-10 minutes, until peppers are tender. 1. Add remanining beans and a drizzle of oil in a small sauce pan over medium heat. 1. Add 1/4 cup black bean liquid and bring to a simmer. 6. Add 1 tablespoon of butter and cook until melted. 1. Using a silicone masher, the black beans and butter until smooth. 1. Spread black bean mixture over tortillas and evenly split filling between tortillas. Roll up tortillas and place them in a 9 x 13 baking dish. 1. Top with salsa verde and monterey jack cheese. Bake at 450 for 5-10 minutes until cheese melts and starts to brown. 1. Combine sour cream and 1/2 tsp southwest seasoning. Mix in 1 teaspoon of water at a time until sauce can be drizzled. Season with salt and pepper to taste. 1. Serve enchiladas with sauce and green onion greens. \normalsize \newpage # Recipe Two \normalsize 1. Cut potatoes into 1/2 inch pieces. Finely chop the onion, celery, and dill. 1. In a stock pot, melt three tablespoons of butter and cook onions and celery for about five minutes. 1. Stir in 1 tablespoon of flour and cook 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently. 1. Continue stirring while slowly adding two cups of water. 1. Add potatoes, mascarpone, and miso. 1. Bring to a simmer over high heat, then lower to medium and cook for 15 minutes. 1. Remove skin from salmon and cut into bite-sized pieces. Stir salmon into the chowder and cook for 4-5 minutes. 1. Remove from heat and stir in chopped dill. Season with salt and pepper to taste. \normalsize \newpage # Recipe Three \normalsize 1. Chop onion and mince garlic. 1. Boil and cook pasta according to package instructions, reserve 1 cup pasta water if it finishes before step 5. 1. Cook onion and garlic in 2 tablespoons olive oil until translucent, 5-7 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, Italian seasoning, and chili flakes. 1. Add frozen peas to onions and garlic. 1. Add two ladles of pasta water to onion, garlic, and peas and simmer. 1. Add drained pasta back into the stock pot, drizzle with olive oil, and season with Italian seasoning. 1. Mix onion, garlic, and peas into the stock pot with pasta. Mix in parmesan cheese. 1. Serve with additional parmesan cheese. \normalsize \newpage # Breakfast Recipe \normalsize 1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees and grease muffin pan. 1. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl. 1. Whisk avocado oil, egg, milk, and vanilla in a medium bowl. 1. Mix the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and then gently stir in the berries. 1. Divide the batter between muffin cups and bake 15-20 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 207 or a toothpick comes out clean. \normalsize
